fields.summary: Jenkins Maven builds failing to download dependencies from Nexus after password expiry
fields.description: All Maven builds on Jenkins started failing overnight with: Could not transfer artifact from/to nexus-releases: Return code is 401. The Nexus repository is accessible via browser. The Maven settings.xml in Jenkins uses a service account nexus-ci-user whose password expired last night according to the 90-day password policy. This is the same root cause as the SonarQube token expiry issue we had last month - service account credentials expiring and breaking CI pipelines.

content.text: This is the third time in 6 months that a service account expiry has taken down CI. First it was SonarQube token, then GitLab runner token, now Nexus. We need a proper solution for service account credential management not just fixing each one individually.

Reset the nexus-ci-user password and updated Maven settings.xml in Jenkins. All builds passing now. Sunita is right - this is a systemic cross-tool problem. Root cause is that all our CI service accounts are subject to the same 90-day password expiry policy designed for human accounts. Fix: raise a request with IT security to exempt CI service accounts from password expiry or implement a secrets rotation automation using HashiCorp Vault.
